title: Migration of IncludeScript
breadcrumbs: Special Considerations When Migrating from Uniface 9
text: Migration of IncludeScript Uniface 9 Include Procs are automatically migrated to IncludeScript objects in an IncludeScript library. When compiling migrated components that contain #include directives, it is possible for the following error to occur: Info: 1000 (Preprocessor) #INCLUDE IncludeProcName Not Found In Uniface 9, the maximum length of Include Proc names and library names was 16 bytes. If a name was longer than this, it was automatically truncated. For example, given an Include Proc called VeryLongLibraryName:VeryLongIncludeProcName , Uniface 9 would truncate it to VeryLongLibraryN.VeryLongIncludeP . The following #include instruction would succeed because it would look for the truncated name: Explain this code #include VeryLongLibraryName:VeryLongIncludeProcName Copy After migration to Uniface 10, the same instruction fails, because it is looking to include VeryLongLibraryName:VeryLongIncludeProcName but the migrated IncludeScript is still called VeryLongLibraryN:VeryLongIncludeP . To correct the problem you my either rename the IncludeScript (and/or IncludeLibrary) or specify the truncated name in the #include instruction. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
